cot_prefix: Okay, let's read through the passage and figure out what the police are asking the public to do. The passage starts by saying that the Sri Lankan police made an appeal for the public to donate their pet dogs to help fight terrorism and crime. So the main request is for people to give their dogs to the police. The notice mentions specific breeds and age requirements, and they want dogs that can be trained for various tasks like detecting explosives and searching for mines. The police have already received some donations, but not all the dogs met the criteria. They mention that they need more dogs because their kennels are under pressure and usually import half of their annual needs. They're making a public appeal for the first time to get more dogs, aiming for about 50 more. The donated dogs after training will become Police Dogs. The answer choices are (A) Offer their dogs to be Police Dogs, (B) Stop their dogs from biting people, (C) Take care not to lose their dogs, (D) Give their dogs good training. Looking at
